pages.full_text:
June 3rd.

    We pack up at Dinas and set off for home.  We go north & have lunch at Celyn reservoir.
    On the way we pass some good views of Harlech Castle.  We also notice a good campsite on a promontary above Harlech - Merthyr Campsite.  A working farm with cows, __beautiful__ views across to Port Merion, very windy.  2½ mile walk down to the sea.  We may try it next year.
    In the afternoon we visit the highest waterfall in Wales, before reaching home.
pages.notes: Year not stated on page; cannot confirm from content alone. 'promontary' is the diary's spelling (sic). 'Port Merion' likely refers to Portmeirion, Wales — transcribed as written. 'Merthyr Campsite' could alternatively read 'Merthyr' as part of a place name but transcribed verbatim. The word 'beautiful' is underlined in the original.

pages.full_text:
June 23rd.

    Simon picked up Peter, Thomas & William from school & drove over to Long Buckby to pick up John, Charles & Eve from the station.  (We'd spent the afternoon visiting Rosemary Young.)
    After about 50 minutes more in the van we arrived at __Cherry Tree Farm__ **** campsite, Woodford Halse, which is within view of the Daventry post office tower.
    There is no pond here, superb views, a large field, no loos, hedges & trees, quite windy.  Peter lost his boomerang as soon as we arrived.  The farm house & walled
pages.notes: Year not stated on page; cannot confirm from content alone. 'Cherry Tree Farm' is underlined in the original, followed by what appear to be four asterisks (****) possibly indicating a star rating. Text cuts off at bottom of page: '...The farm house & walled' — continues on next spread. 'loos' means toilets (British slang).
